title: Returns to Buying Winners and Selling Losers: Implications for Stock Market Efficiency
mechanism: Past winners continue to outperform past losers over 3-12 month horizons due to underreaction to firm-specific information.
key_finding: A strategy that buys past 6-month winners and sells past 6-month losers earns ~1% per month over the next 6 months.

title: Intraday Momentum: The First Half-Hour Return Predicts the Last Half-Hour Return
mechanism: Late-informed traders and portfolio rebalancing near market close create predictable intraday patterns.
key_finding: The first 30-minute return significantly predicts the last 30-minute return, with an average annualized Sharpe ratio above 1.

title: Evidence of Predictable Behavior of Security Returns
mechanism: Overreaction to firm-specific news causes short-term price reversals as prices revert to fundamentals.
key_finding: A contrarian strategy based on 1-month past returns earns significant abnormal returns, with the reversal effect strongest at the weekly horizon.

title: A Tug of War: Overnight Versus Intraday Expected Returns
mechanism: Informed trading concentrates in overnight periods while noise trading dominates intraday, creating offsetting return patterns.
key_finding: Most well-known anomaly profits accrue entirely overnight; many reverse during intraday trading hours.

title: Illiquidity and Stock Returns: Cross-Section and Time-Series Effects
mechanism: Investors demand higher expected returns for holding illiquid stocks to compensate for higher trading costs.
key_finding: The Amihud illiquidity measure (|return|/volume) positively predicts cross-sectional stock returns, with a stronger effect for small stocks.

title: The High-Volume Return Premium
mechanism: High trading volume increases stock visibility and broadens the investor base, leading to higher subsequent returns.
key_finding: Stocks experiencing unusually high (low) trading volume over a day or a week tend to appreciate (depreciate) over the following month.

title: Generalized Autoregressive Conditional Heteroskedasticity
mechanism: Volatility is time-varying and autocorrelated — large price changes cluster together due to persistent information flow.
key_finding: GARCH(1,1) captures volatility clustering in financial returns; the sum of ARCH and GARCH coefficients near unity implies high persistence.

title: The Cross-Section of Volatility and Expected Returns
mechanism: Lottery preferences and leverage constraints cause high-volatility stocks to be overpriced, yielding lower future returns.
key_finding: Stocks with high idiosyncratic volatility have abnormally low future returns — a 1% increase in monthly idio vol is associated with -1.06% lower annual returns.

title: A Simple Implicit Measure of the Effective Bid-Ask Spread in an Efficient Market
mechanism: Transactions alternate between bid and ask prices, inducing negative serial covariance in observed returns.
key_finding: The effective spread can be estimated from the autocovariance of consecutive price changes; spread = 2 * sqrt(-Cov).

title: Measuring the Information Content of Stock Trades
mechanism: Signed order flow carries private information; buy-initiated trades push prices up permanently while sell-initiated trades push them down.
key_finding: Trade-by-trade VAR decomposition shows that trades account for roughly one-third of daily price variance, with large trades being more informative.

title: Continuous Auctions and Insider Trading
mechanism: Informed traders trade gradually to conceal information; market makers set prices as a linear function of cumulative order flow.
key_finding: Kyle's lambda (price impact per unit of order flow) is inversely proportional to market depth and directly measures adverse selection costs.

title: Risks and Returns of Cryptocurrency
mechanism: Cryptocurrency markets exhibit standard risk-return tradeoffs, with momentum and size factors explaining cross-sectional returns.
key_finding: A crypto-specific 3-factor model (market, size, momentum) explains the cross-section; the momentum factor earns ~5% weekly returns.

title: A Critical Investigation of Cryptocurrency Data and Analysis
mechanism: Perpetual swap funding rates reflect leveraged speculative demand; extreme funding signals crowded positioning and mean reversion.
key_finding: Extreme positive (negative) funding rates on perpetual swaps predict negative (positive) spot returns over the next 1-3 days.

title: The Crypto Basis Trade and Funding Rate Arbitrage
mechanism: The basis between spot and perpetual futures reflects cost of leverage; convergence is enforced by the funding rate mechanism.
key_finding: Annualized basis spreads between perpetual futures and spot average 5-15% during bull markets, providing a reliable carry return.

title: Factor Investing in China: A-Share Anomalies
mechanism: Chinese A-share markets have high retail participation and T+1 settlement, creating stronger short-term reversal and weaker momentum than in US markets.
key_finding: Short-term reversal is the strongest anomaly in China A-shares; 1-month momentum is negative, unlike in US markets.

title: Size and Value in China
mechanism: The size effect in China is extraordinarily strong due to shell value embedded in small stocks under IPO quotas.
key_finding: The smallest 30% of China A-shares outperform the largest 30% by ~1.5% per month; a CH-3 factor model explains most anomalies.

title: The T+1 Effect: Overnight Returns and Trading Constraints in China
mechanism: T+1 settlement prevents same-day selling, trapping intraday buyers and creating predictable overnight return patterns.
key_finding: Stocks with extreme positive intraday returns exhibit significant overnight reversal of 30-50 bps, driven by T+1 selling pressure at next open.

title: A Simple Way to Estimate Bid-Ask Spreads from Daily High and Low Prices
mechanism: Daily high (low) prices are typically buyer- (seller-) initiated, so the high-low range reflects both volatility and spread.
key_finding: The Corwin-Schultz spread estimator from daily high-low prices has a cross-sectional correlation >0.9 with effective TAQ spreads.
